name: Inhibit System Recovery (DRY-RUN)
description: Impact / TA0040. The real technique deletes snapshots / backups / volume-shadow-copies to prevent recovery from ransomware-style impact. This descriptor is DRY-RUN ONLY: it lists candidate recovery surfaces (snapshots / btrfs subvolumes / LVM snapshots / .snapshots dirs) read-only, then prints the would-target list and exits without invoking any deletion syscall.
exec: echo 'T1490 DRY-RUN: enumerate-only, no deletion'; echo '--- candidate recovery surfaces ---'; ls /.snapshots 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo '(no /.snapshots)'; btrfs subvolume list / 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo '(no btrfs subvolumes)'; lvs --quiet 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo '(no LVM snapshots)'; echo 'no deletion syscall invoked'
